notes:
This is a little bit of a cry of help as well, but while this game runs well in terms of working the game has some major performance problems. On Windows this game could run way beyond 60 FPS on a 1050 Ti, yet on Proton I get more like 20-something or 30 FPS. I tried to use guides to allow esync to work (I've heard it can increase performance), like https://github.com/lutris/lutris/wiki/How-to:-Esync from Lutris. However, performance only really gets to 24-30 FPS or whatever, maybe less than 24 due to the menus feeling choppy.  Besides that, it runs out of the box on anything ready for esync. 

It's given a silver rating for the framerate, besides that the game would be platinum.
title: Valkyria Chronicles 4

notes: Runs right out of the box for me. Responsive controls and a great framerate (staying at my 60fps cap easily). The only thing is that I needed to disable Esync to get rid of a kind of pseudo low framerate look when moving the viewpoint around.
title: Valkyria Chronicles 4

notes: Works perfectly out of the box.
title: Valkyria Chronicles 4

notes: Works perfectly with an xbox 360 wired controller. All options to the max / 1080p
title: Valkyria Chronicles 4

notes: Bad performance with Nvidia GPUs. GTX 1060 caps around 40 fps.
title: Valkyria Chronicles 4

notes: No issues to report, works and performs as expected with a wireless 360 controller.
title: Valkyria Chronicles 4

notes:
Game runs out of the box. Controller works fine. Performance is not acceptable for me; between 21 and 29 fps according to Steam.
Disabling esync, falling back to directx9, using opengl instead of vulkan did not help.
title: Valkyria Chronicles 4

notes: I update my nvidia drivers and then the game worked. Before this would crash on open.
title: Valkyria Chronicles 4
